content: The spelling variant 'reflexion' (the less common British English spelling of reflection from high school vocabulary curricula) can be framed as a 'minor inconsistent perspective' that mirrors the core insight of Self-Contrast LLM self-reflection: small divergent representations of the same core concept help agents surface flawed reasoning more effectively than uniform perspectives. This suggests we can improve LLM self-reflection by intentionally introducing controlled minor variants of problem framing, just as different spelling variants reveal the same underlying concept through subtly different representations.
